feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Native way to list "In-Use" Dimensions/Measures for Dashboard Documentation?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working on a project to centralize the documentation of our analytics assets. I need to list the technical components (Dimensions, Measures, and Filters) that are actually used within each of our SAC Stories.

The goal is to provide users with a clear view of what data is represented in each dashboard without them having to open the Story and investigate the widgets.

The Issue: Currently, I find it very difficult to get this information automatically. A Data Model or the Queries might contain 50 dimensions, but a specific Story might only use 5 of them. I need to identify those 5 "active" dimensions. Same for Measures or Filters.

Manually opening every Story and clicking on every Chart/Table to check the "Builder" panel is not a viable option for us due to the number of dashboards we manage.

My Questions:

  1. Is there a native feature or a standard report in SAC that provides a summary of the objects (Dimensions/Measures) actually placed in a Story’s widgets?
  2. Does the SAC Catalog offer a way to display these specific "In-Use" technical metadata in the overview or discovery tabs?
  3. Is there any "Usage" or "Impact Analysis" view that works from the Story level down to the Dimension level, rather than just from the Model level?

I am looking for a standard method within the interface to avoid manual auditing.

Thank you for your insights!

entry.title: Can I export javascript code from SAC story
entry.content:
Hi

We would like to export SAC story javascript code to separate file.

Is this possible?

entry.title: Deprecated Classic History
entry.content: The problem is that classic stories need functionality that optimized stories don't have.

entry.title: Default SAP Identity Provider (IdP) Behavior for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Hello!

I would like to get a better understanding on how the default SAP Identity Provider (IdP) works for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

We are currently facing an issue with SSO for SAP Analytics Cloud, and we have reverted to the default IdP from a custom IdP. Since then I am not able to log in to SAP Analytics Cloud (with my SAP credentials) and I get the 'It seems that you don't have an active account' page. 

I have verified my account with https://hanacloudservices-eu.accounts.ondemand.com/ and have also reset my password. The credentials I set during the authentication are aligned with the details displayed in Profile Management (https://hanacloudservices-eu.accounts.ondemand.com/).

It also seems that some users are able to log in without any issues using their SAP credentials.

Any insights into how the default SAP IdP for SAP Analytics Cloud functions and what might be causing this issue would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks, and best regards,

CS

entry.title: Issue while creating the measure input control
entry.content:
While creating the measure input control. The error is coming stating "The query was stopped before it finished because the server was unable to complete the request. Please try filtering your data, or contacting your administrator."

Due to this error I am unable to apply measure based filter into the chart.

entry.title: Failed to create Live Connection at SAP SAC to SAP HANA Cloud for FinOps
entry.content:
Fehler bei der Verifizierung einer Live Connection in SAC (eu20) auf eine SAP HANA Cloud (eu30).

Allow all IP addresses ist in der HANA Instanz gesetzt. Trotzdem scheitert das anlegen der Connection

Anleitung ist von SAP https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q0X_XF1QuAg

https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-resource-consumption-monitor

Die Fehler-Meldung ist sehr unspezifisch.  

We couldn't connect to your HANA system. For more information, see our troubleshooting page.
Correlation ID: 8502223582444492b974251792547832

entry.title: Errors when trying to import a csv file into a model
entry.content:
The aim is to import 2 csv files into a model (one with prod and the other one with test data), with same columns, and compare the values for one column, against the other columns.

First I want to import one the 2 files, and right after the import step I can see 2 errors, maybe linked :

1) for some lines imported, the date value of the column "Date de début" (= begin date) is highlighted in red by SAC. However, the date format for the file and the one for the imported data are always OK

- file date format = dd.mm.yyyy, and imported data format = mm/dd/yyyy
- for the highlighted cells, the error message returned is "Invalid Date Format"

2) the same column "Date de début" has been generated by default as a Date dimension with hierarchical levels (Year, Quarter, month, Day) and it seems to be impossible to turn this column into a standard Generic dimension, 
as all other columns has been generated into. For the comparison step (with the 2nd file imported), I need to have all the columns as Generic dimensions, with the amount column as the only measure.

I send you some printscreens for help (attached file)

Could you please explain to me how to resolve these 2 errors ?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Is it possible to show only leaves in SAC Excel add-in table?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I can show only leaf members in a table in an SAC story. Is it also possible to show only leaf members in an SAC Excel Add‑in table? I don’t seem to see a “Show Leaves Only” option in Excel. The reason is that I only want the date to display 202501, 202502, 202503, 202504, …, 202512. However, the Excel Add‑in shows ALL – 2025 – 202501, 202502, 202503, 202504, …, 202512. It seems that in Excel, it’s not possible to display only the month level.

Has anyone met similar problems?

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance.

SAP Analytics Cloud 

---------- oh i found the select leaf members only option in the filter panel... Thank you.

entry.title: SAC Planning Master Data Mapping for Cost Category (Major/Minor) based on Usage × Account
entry.content:
Background / Current Setup

We are using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Planning with an import model.

There is an existing source planning model already in use. For operational reasons, we do not want to redesign or modify the source model directly. Instead, we refresh a separate target planning model for analysis using Data Action – Cross Model Copy (manual trigger is acceptable).

Our fact data includes these dimensions:

  • Usage Code
  • Account Code

For reporting, we need additional classification dimensions:

  • Cost Minor Category
  • Cost Major Category (parent level of Cost Minor)

The classification is defined by an Excel mapping table:

  • (Usage Code, Account Code) → Cost Minor Category → Cost Major Category

We also have Data Integration in SAC, but our tenant does not provide the ETL-style Data Flows feature.

Requirements

  1. Populate classification dimensions
  • The combination Usage Code × Account Code uniquely determines the cost classification.
  • After refreshing data into the target model, we want to populate the Cost Minor Category dimension based on that two-key mapping.
  • Cost Major Category should be available via a Major → Minor hierarchy.
  1. Story reporting
  • In Stories, we want to use Cost Major Category as an axis and drill down to Cost Minor Category.
  • Therefore, Cost Major/Minor must exist as dimensions (not just properties).
  1. Refresh operation After the source model is updated, we want a manual process that:
  • runs Cross Model Copy to refresh the target model, and
  • then applies the mapping so the Cost Category dimensions are filled correctly.

Key Challenges / Questions

  1. Recommended modeling approach Because the mapping depends on the combination of two dimensions, it cannot be modeled consistently as a property on Usage Code alone or Account Code alone.
  • Is it recommended/required to create a composite key dimension such as UsageAccountKey = Usage Code + Account Code?
  • Or is there a better SAC Planning modeling pattern to represent a two-key classification?
  1. How to apply the mapping without Data Flows Since Data Flows are not available in our tenant, we cannot perform an ETL-style join in SAC Data Integration.
  • What is the recommended way to store the Excel mapping table in SAC (e.g., as a dimension, a small mapping model, a dataset, etc.)?
  • Can a Data Action (or Advanced Formulas / Data Action steps) perform a lookup to populate a dimension (Cost Minor Category) based on (Usage Code, Account Code)?
  1. Best practice architecture with Cross Model Copy Given a process centered on Cross Model Copy:
  • Should the mapping be applied before the copy (source side), after the copy (target side), or via an intermediate model?
  • What is the best-practice approach to keep the target model reportable with stable Major → Minor drill-down?

What We Are Looking For

A recommended architecture / best practice for SAC Planning import models to:

  • enrich fact data with a classification dimension derived from two keys,
  • support hierarchy-based aggregation and drill-down in Stories,
  • while keeping the source model unchanged and using Cross Model Copy for refresh.

entry.title: How to display only existing dimension combination only in SAC excel add-in?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

I’m facing an issue related to data combinations and would appreciate any advice.

In my case, I have the following dimensions:

  • Product Category: 20 values
  • Product Type: 70 values
  • Material: 400 values

If no rules are applied, I'll get 20 * 70 * 400 = 560,000 rows of results. However, only about 500 rows are actually valid and maintained in the business context. The data will be displayed by month. With 12 months, the total would be 560,000 × 12, which seems too large.

 

I tried to define some validation rules in the model, and they seem to work fine for display purposes when I select the “Hide unplannable and unbooked data” option and restrict the product category to a small set. 

However, when I open the full data in the Excel Add-In, I’m not able to open the table. Instead, I get the following error message:

“The data can’t be displayed because too many records are returned. Please try to reduce the amount of data by collapsing hierarchies, removing dimensions, or applying filters.”

I’m not sure why this error occurs. Does this mean that Excel is still counting the cells that are hidden by the hide unplannable unbooked data option when retrieving the data?

I’m also unsure whether using validation rules is the right approach to control and display valid combinations across three dimensions. Does anyone have a better approach or best practice for modeling or displaying only valid dimension combinations in this scenario?

Any suggestions or insights would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

(I attached a screenshot for illustration.)

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Is it possible to have multiple SAP cloud connections to the same SAP instance?
entry.content:
Hi, 

There is a SAP Cloud connector between SAC and BW4.

Is it possible to have multiple SAP cloud connections to the same SAP instance (two SAP Cloud connections between SAC and BW4)

Thanks

entry.title: geo coordinates from address
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm struggling with geo coordinates from SAP in SAC.

The best way to do it seems to be:

1) create the two geo columns ( latitude and longitude) in the model

2) Create the two dimensions using Colab and Excel file with full address.

2) populate each customer with the two values pre-calculated

That's good but not very simple and easy.

Is there someone who knows any other solution that works for all the addresses in the world like Google?

tks

entry.title: SAC reports to live HANA prompting for user name and password
entry.content: we have SAC reports which is configured with HANA live connection, reports keeps asking for hana user and password, please advise how can we avoid the prompt

entry.title: SAC calculated measure ( with 0 instead off null) in filter panel doesn't work
entry.content:
Measure ( REV_CY) has rows with null value and , as I cannot filter it , I created a new measure where write 0 instead to null.
Is there a reason why I continue to not see the lines with 0 value calculated ?

 

Lines with "VAL FATT AC" equal ro null but copied in  REV_CY_FILTER wiht 0 are not showed

tks

entry.title: How to make story NOT planning enabled by default
entry.content:
Stories created by users on a planning model are set to planning enabled by default.

We have a requirement where a Team of users need the ability to create reporting stories on a planning model. The Team members that are not Planning users cannot publish any data in these stories as expected, however users that have planning roles are able to publish data in the user created stories, as the story is "Planning Enabled" when created. We are looking for a way to ensure the user created stories on a planning model are not able to be used to enter data by a planning user. The planning users do enter data in “authorised” planning stories as part of the planning process.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Duration calculation Between Two Dates
entry.content: I have employee event history data with multiple records for the event reason “Promotion - Annual Pay Change” for the same employee. I need to calculate the duration between the latest Start Date of the promotion event and the Last End date or till now. Since there are multiple promotion records, I want SAC to dynamically pick the most recent Start Date and calculate the duration.

entry.title: How to display multiple measures in SAC pie/donut chart?
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

It seems that only one measure is allowed for SAC pie/donut chart according to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/41836ac6c47d4a96987622a7ecc422b0.html?q=pie+chart#donut-or-pie-charts.

I am wondering whether there is some workaround to do so....as I have three measures and would like to combine their total in one pie/donut chart. A screenshot has been attached for illustration.

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated! Thank you in advance.

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: S4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD CDS VIEWS for FP&A Planning
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are currently implementing SAC FP&A Planning for our client, involving the following system landscape: S4HANA Public Cloud, Datasphere, and SAC for Planning. The planning scenarios we are focusing on include Opex, Capex, Revenue, and P&L.

We have established the following communication arrangements:

  • SAP_COM_0002
  • SAP_COM_0008
  • SAP_COM_0532
  • SAP_COM_0065
  • SAP_COM_0087

However, we are facing challenges in identifying the appropriate CDS views for transaction data. I have attempted to utilize the following CDS views from the SAP portal, but they are unavailable for extraction:

  • I_JournalEntryItem → Full line-item detail
  • I_ActualPlanJournalEntryItem → Actual + Plan
  • I_GLAccountLineItemCube → Optimized analytical line items

Could you please advise if there are any communication arrangements we may have overlooked or how we can enable data extraction? Additionally, I would appreciate a list of CDS views we should use for both Master data and Transaction data to effectively carry out the FP&A Planning, espe

entry.title: Unable to call Parameterized ODATA services in the build query panel in SAC
entry.content:
We have custom CDS query (ODATA) which has parameters and we need to access that in SAC as an import data source.
Business doesn't want to use freehand query please advise how to achieve this.

Thanks
Arjun

entry.title: Establish Connection Between SAC and On-Premise HANA DB
entry.content:
We want to Establish the Connection Between SAC and On-Premise HANA DB. We are referred the below link. We are getting below error. 

We couldn't connect to your HANA system. For more information, see our troubleshooting page.TroubleshootCorrelation ID: 30303874649147778769255325863782Copy to Clipboard

Live Data Connection to SAP HANA On-Premise Using a Tunnel Connection | SAP Help Portal
